event: found to have Clostridium difficile infection
abdominal and pelvic computer tomography (CT) scan
hypoenhancing area in the mid appendix
new finding of an adjacent loop of small bowel with thickened wall
no extraluminal air or drainable fluid collections
taken urgently to the operating room for an appendectomy
operation began with a laparoscopic approach
appendix was found to be completely necrotic
lying on top of an adjacent loop of terminal ileum
segmentally necrotic at the contacting surface
performed an ileocecectomy with primary stapled anastomosis
fascia was closed but the skin was left open
admitted to the intensive care unit postoperatively
ischemic changes, hemorrhage, and thrombosed vessels
filled with broad irregular aseptate hyphae
involving both vessels and adjacent submucosa
invading through the muscularis propria of the appendix
antifungal therapy was immediately switched to amphotericin B
fungal overgrowth was found in the surgical wound
culture of the bronchoalveolar lavage recovered Absidia spp.
goals of care were transitioned to comfort
